threshold_definition: Stationary: bias 0.1, LoA -1.09 to +1.19; Mobile: bias 0.0, LoA -1.07 to +1.07
notes: MDR Class IIb validation; stationary values shown; mobile LoA -1.07 to +1.07

threshold_definition: Overall: bias 0.18, LoA -3.06 to +3.42; Hospitalized only (n=53): bias 0.07, LoA -3.84 to +3.97
notes: Validated Medtronic Nellcor CNRRS (pulse-ox derived RR); NOT impedance/capnography device itself

threshold_definition: Accuracy rates: pediatric sleep lab 91.8%; adult sleep lab 93.1%; ICU adult 82% vs ETCO2; 75% vs manual count
notes: NICE MIB49 summary; accuracy defined as aRE within thresholds; no Bland-Altman data available

threshold_definition: Within 10% or 2 bpm of reference (FDA data); Neteera website claims 95%
notes: FDA clearance data; discrepancy between FDA submission (~93%) and marketing material (95%)

threshold_definition: At rest: LoA 1.0 to 1.8 bpm vs capnography
notes: Small sample; limited to resting conditions; LoA interpretation unclear in source

threshold_definition: vs ECG: bias -0.41 LoA [-3.9,3.1]; vs nurse: bias -0.58 LoA [-5.5,4.3]; 20% intervals >3 bpm vs nurse
notes: NICE concluded evidence base is limited; single-center studies

threshold_definition: IPG (impedance) vs capnography; NOTE: 98.9% LoA (not 95%); 95% LoA would be narrower
notes: 98.9% LoA using ±2.63×SD with repeated-measures correction; none of 3 devices met predefined ±2 bpm criterion

journal: J Am Coll Emerg Physicians Open
threshold_definition: Telemetry (impedance) vs capnography: bias 0.2, LoA -6.2 to +6.6; Manual vs capnography: bias -0.6, LoA -13.5 to +12.3
notes: PMC11077426; demonstrates wide LoA for both impedance and manual vs capnography in ED
